category_title: Students in an inclusive urban classroom exhibiting high disruptive behavior and low engagement
minimal_context: The student is expected to work independently on an assigned reading task following a mini-lesson, but needs to navigate the classroom to retrieve supplies from the teacher's desk.
dialogue:
TUTOR: The mini-reading lesson is over, so please start working independently on your assigned reading task.

STUDENT: (Gets out of seat and walks through the congested pathway toward the teacher's desk to get supplies, stopping to stare at the computer screens facing the class)

TUTOR: Please sit down at your desk and work on the assigned task.

STUDENT: (Shows noncompliance to the teacher direction, making unwanted physical contact by bumping into a peer in the pathway) Move out of my way!

TUTOR: We need to go back to our seats and focus on our own work.

STUDENT: (Pushes the peer, throws a pencil across the room, and calls out a profanity, showing complete disregard for the teacher's instruction)

options.A: Ask open-ended questions about the assigned reading task so that the student can write about what they already know.
options.B: Provide large fonts with supporting pictures, reduce the number of reading questions, and allow access to word banks and speech-generating devices.
options.C: Use desk carrels (referred to as their 'office') for independent work during the assigned reading task.
options.D: Establish a classroom norm where disagreement is acceptable and provide a platform for the evidence-based exchange of viewpoints.
